title: polemonium viscosum
intro: Polemonium viscosum, also known as sticky Jacob's-ladder, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its unique, sticky stems and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Polemonium viscosum include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a variety of health issues.
